title: Set the Vision
subtitle: Where should the company be in 2027?

payload.prompt: Strategic Cloud Driver or Weak Driver?

cards.label: We want faster business decisions based on real-time data.
We only move because ECC support is ending.
We want to reduce dependency on outdated, heavily customized systems.
We want to keep every current process exactly as it is.
We want a more scalable IT setup for future growth or new sites.

title: Build the Decision Gate
subtitle: Choose the five evidence items a CEO should require before approving the next migration phase.

payload.prompt: What evidence do you need before approving the next step toward SAP S/4HANA Cloud?

options.label: Business case with cost, expected value, and payback logic
Downtime tolerance per critical business area
Department risk overview from Finance, IT, Production, and HR
Migration path comparison: greenfield, brownfield, or selective transition
Clear go/no-go responsibility and escalation process
Only a technical recommendation from IT
A project name and internal campaign logo
Waiting until deadline pressure forces a decision
Assuming cloud automatically reduces all costs

title: Prioritize the Risks
subtitle: Rank the risks from most business-critical to least business-critical.

payload.prompt: Rank from most critical (Top) to least critical (Bottom)

items.label: No tested rollback plan if go-live fails
Production downtime tolerance not confirmed
Critical financial reports not validated yet
Some employees are unhappy about the new interface

title: Business Case in 90 Seconds
subtitle: Match each CEO fear with the strongest business argument or control mechanism.

payload.prompt: Match Fear to Argument

items.label: "Will this only create cost?"
"Will operations stop?"
"Will we lose control over data?"
"Will employees resist?"
"What is the strategic value?"

targets.label: Lower long-term maintenance effort and better cost transparency can improve the business case.
A controlled migration path, dry runs, fallback planning, and phased rollout reduce business continuity risk.
Data validation, ownership, backup, and migration testing make data risk visible and controllable.
Early key-user involvement and role-specific training reduce change resistance.
Real-time insights, scalability, and future-proof processes support faster and better decisions.

title: Send a Booster
subtitle: One department is behind in the challenge. Choose the best leadership message.

questions.prompt: Which message should the CEO send to motivate a lagging department?

options.label: "Please finish this quickly, we need the points."
"Your input is important because your department sees risks management cannot see from the top."
"IT will solve the cloud topic for everyone."
"If you do not participate, the score will look bad."

questions.explanation: A CEO needs to emphasize the business-criticality of the department's input.

title: Report Rescue
subtitle: Month-end closing! Protect reporting by dragging each report to the right category.

payload.prompt: Drag each report into the right category

items.label: Balance Sheet
Profit & Loss Statement
Cost Center Report
Cash Flow Forecast
Tax Report
Sales Dashboard

title: Cloud Myth or Finance Fact?
subtitle: Swipe each statement to Myth or Fact.

payload.prompt: Myth or Fact?

cards.label: Real-time reporting can reduce waiting time for financial insights.
SAP Cloud automatically solves all finance problems without process changes.
Manual Excel workarounds can make migration and reporting more complex.
Compliance and audit trails are only relevant after go-live.
Finance should be involved early, not only shortly before go-live.

title: First Closing After Go-Live
subtitle: Rank the risks from most critical to least critical for the first month-end closing.

payload.prompt: Sort from most critical (Top) to least critical (Bottom)

items.label: Incomplete financial postings
Missing audit trail
Users unsure about new closing steps
Dashboard layout looks different

title: Pain Point → Cloud Benefit
subtitle: Match today's Finance pain point with the most relevant SAP Cloud/S/4HANA benefit.

payload.prompt: Match Pain Point to Benefit

items.label: Manual Excel consolidation
Slow management reports
Difficult reconciliation between FI and CO
Unclear cost transparency
High effort for system maintenance

title: Create the Finance Migration Shield
subtitle: Choose 4 out of 8 shield parts that best protect Finance during migration.

payload.prompt: Select the correct actions to build your shield:

options.label: Test first month-end closing in a dry run
Wait until after go-live to check reports
Let only IT validate finance data

title: ROI Quick Builder
subtitle: Enter rough values to estimate your cloud potential.

title: Custom Code Reality Check
subtitle: Find out whether the company understands its custom code landscape and whether it is ready for a clean-core migration.

questions.prompt: How many custom Z/Y objects exist in your ECC system?
questions.explanation: A high number of Z/Y objects increases migration complexity, especially if usage and ownership are unclear.

questions.prompt: Do you know which custom objects are actually used?

options.label: Yes, based on usage statistics / monitoring / SCMON

questions.explanation: Usage data is critical. Without it, old custom code may be migrated unnecessarily.

questions.prompt: How many custom developments modify or enhance SAP standard behavior?

options.label: Very few — mostly reports, forms, or external add-ons
Some exits, BADIs, enhancements, or user exits exist
Many core process modifications / enhancements exist

questions.explanation: Enhancements close to SAP standard processes are critical because they may conflict with clean-core principles.

questions.prompt: How often do you clean up unused custom code?
questions.explanation: Code cleanup before migration reduces technical debt and lowers testing effort.

title: Cloud Fit Sorting
subtitle: Sort technical SAP objects into the correct cloud strategy.

payload.prompt: Sort technical SAP objects into the correct cloud strategy:

items.label: Z-report used daily for legal reporting, no standard alternative
Custom approval workflow similar to SAP Flexible Workflow
Old Z-transaction not used in 24 months
Custom program with direct database update
Interface using local file path and no owner
Duplicate custom report with same logic as standard SAP analytics
Critical warehouse interface with owner, documentation, monitoring
Modification in SAP standard object
Custom Fiori app built on released API
Old batch job with unknown business owner
Custom table storing historical data only
Enhancement in order-to-cash process without documentation

title: Interface Risk Radar
subtitle: Identify which integrations are cloud-ready and which ones may become migration blockers.

payload.prompt: Identify interface scenario risk level:

items.label: API-based interface, documented owner, monitored errors
IDoc interface used daily, owner known, documentation partly outdated
File-based interface via local server path, no owner, business-critical
External system directly reads ECC database tables
Middleware-managed interface with logging and retry mechanism
Manual Excel upload used for monthly postings
MES interface with no fallback process
Standard BAPI-based integration with monitoring
RFC connection used by multiple systems, owner unclear
OData service used by Fiori app with proper authorization concept
Interface depends on custom table with unknown data owner
Batch interface with no error notification

title: Clean Core Decision Gate
subtitle: Test whether IT understands SAP clean-core principles and cloud-native migration decisions.

questions.prompt: A custom approval workflow exists, but S/4HANA Cloud offers SAP Flexible Workflow. What should you do first?

options.label: Compare business requirements with SAP standard workflow and standardize where possible

questions.explanation: Cloud migration is a chance to replace custom logic with standard SAP capabilities.

questions.prompt: A business-critical Z-report is used daily, but no owner is defined. Best next step?

options.label: Assign owner, check usage, document logic, and evaluate SAP standard alternative

questions.explanation: Every critical custom object needs ownership and a target decision.

questions.prompt: An external system reads directly from ECC database tables. Main issue for cloud migration?

options.label: Direct database access may not fit cloud architecture and creates dependency risk

questions.explanation: Cloud-ready integrations should use released APIs, services, middleware, or standard integration patterns.

questions.prompt: Which extension approach is most aligned with clean core?

options.label: Use side-by-side extensions, released APIs, SAP BTP, or in-app extensibility where possible

questions.explanation: Clean core does not mean “no extensions.” It means extensions should avoid modifying the SAP core.

questions.prompt: Which action improves readiness before moving from ECC/on-premise to S/4HANA Cloud?

options.label: Identify custom code usage, interfaces, data volume, roles, critical dependencies, and simplification impacts
Freeze all business processes without analysis

questions.explanation: Technical readiness needs a system-wide view: code, data, integration, authorization, testing, and operations.

title: Cloud Operations Readiness Check
subtitle: Check whether the IT team has the operational basics for a controlled cloud transition.

payload.prompt: Select all items that are essential before moving from ECC/on-premise toward S/4HANA Cloud:

options.label: SAP Readiness Check / technical assessment completed
ATC checks for custom code compatibility
Interface inventory with owner and criticality
Defined interface monitoring and error handling
Business process testing with key users
Operations model clarified: what stays internal, what moves to SAP / cloud provider
Keep all roles unchanged without review
Move all custom developments without usage check
Let IT test alone without business users
Assume all Z-code will work unchanged
Check integrations only during go-live weekend

title: Bottleneck Tagging
subtitle: From the material flow, tag the step that hiccups/annoys daily.

payload.prompt: Smooth or Bottleneck?

cards.label: Order entry
Availability check
Production scheduling
Goods receipt
Shipping & paperwork

title: Downtime Check
subtitle: Which process tolerates standstill the least? Sort from Least Tolerant (Top) to Most Tolerant (Bottom).

payload.prompt: Sort from Least Tolerant to Standstill (Top) to Most (Bottom)

items.label: Assembly Line
Packaging
Quality Control
Warehouse Picking

title: Real-time Potential
subtitle: Find hidden SAP migration risks before they become go-live problems.

node-start.bot: Where do you most urgently need live data to improve production operations?

node-downtime.bot: How much unplanned downtime do you estimate per week?

node-quality.bot: What is your approximate scrap/rework rate?

node-order.bot: How often is the production plan not aligned with actual shopfloor status?

node-material.bot: How often do you experience material shortages that halt production?

node-impact-downtime.bot: What is the main operational impact of this downtime?

options.label: High waiting times and low OEE

node-impact-quality.bot: What is the main operational impact of quality defects?

options.label: Extra rework cycles and capacity loss

node-impact-order.bot: What is the main operational impact of misaligned plans?

options.label: Lack of sequence control on the shopfloor

node-impact-material.bot: What is the main operational impact of material shortages?

options.label: Line stops and high setup costs

node-end-downtime.bot:
Real-time potential: Very high.
Main value driver: live machine status monitoring.
Likely SAP relevance: Production order confirmations, shopfloor integration, and real-time downtime tracking.

node-end-quality.bot:
Real-time potential: High.
Main value driver: live quality checks and inspection lots.
Likely SAP relevance: QM integration, in-process inspection, scrap postings, and rework order processing.

node-end-order.bot:
Real-time potential: Very high.
Main value driver: live shopfloor status tracking.
Likely SAP relevance: PP order confirmations, digital kanban, and production scheduling cockpit.

node-end-material.bot:
Real-time potential: Very high.
Main value driver: live material availability.
Likely SAP relevance: PP/MM integration, stock accuracy, goods movements, and real-time shopfloor feedback.

title: SAP PP Risk Radar
subtitle: Identify which SAP production issues are critical during migration.

payload.prompt: Classify each scenario as Low, Medium, or High Risk:

items.label: Production orders are visible, but layout looks different
Stock levels in SAP are inaccurate during go-live
MES interface to SAP is not fully tested
Goods movements are posted with delay
Users are unsure how to confirm production orders
BOM or routing data is outdated
Work center capacities are not maintained correctly
Quality inspection results do not flow back to SAP
Shopfloor dashboard refreshes slowly
Labels / packaging data missing after migration
Production order release works, but old screen looks different
Material staging process has no fallback

title: Build the Process Flow
subtitle: Drag & drop steps into the right order.

payload.prompt: Order the process flow (Start at Top, End at Bottom)

items.label: Receive Materials
Pre-Assembly
Final Assembly
Quality Check
Shipping

title: Change Booster or Blocker?
subtitle: Are these actions helping or hurting the cloud transition?

payload.prompt: Booster or Blocker?

cards.label: Key users are involved early.
Employees hear about migration shortly before go-live.
Training is role-specific and practical.
Communication only uses technical SAP language.
Teams can share concerns anonymously.

title: Training Match
subtitle: Match employee group to best training format.

payload.prompt: Match Group to Training Format

items.label: Shopfloor workers
Key users
Managers
Finance users
IT users

title: Resistance Ranking
subtitle: Rank the causes of resistance from most serious to least serious.

payload.prompt: Rank from most serious (Top) to least serious (Bottom)

items.label: Employees do not understand why the change is happening
Key users are involved too late
Training is too generic
The project has a new name

options.label: "The system will change, but we will show what it means for your daily work and involve key users early."
